touches.ai_summary: Lucas Suzuki submitted an inbound demo request via website form expressing interest in improving forecasting accuracy for their finance operations. Form indicated they're currently using spreadsheets and looking for automation.
next_steps: Rep to respond within 24 hours
Schedule qualification call

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent personalized follow-up email acknowledging LunarLogic's fintech focus and proposing initial call to discuss forecasting challenges. Lucas responded positively and shared availability for next week.

questions_asked.question: Do you have experience with other fintech companies our size?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we work with several SMB fintech clients including two that moved from manual spreadsheet forecasting to automated workflows

next_steps: Qualification call scheduled for Tuesday 2pm

touches.ai_summary: 18-minute qualification call with Lucas covered current forecasting process (manual Excel models updated weekly), pain points around accuracy during market volatility, and team size. Lucas confirmed 4-person finance team would be primary users. Timeline is flexible but would like solution in place before Q2 planning cycle.

questions_asked.question: How long does typical implementation take for a company our size?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 2-3 weeks for core setup, then ongoing refinement. We can prioritize forecasting module to get you running faster

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency around forecasting accuracy right now?
questions_asked.answer: Board wants better visibility into cash flow projections, especially with market uncertainty. Current models are off by 15-20% some months

next_steps: Lucas to map out current forecasting workflow
Rep to send discovery agenda
Discovery call scheduled for next Thursday

touches.ai_summary: 27-minute discovery call dove deep into LunarLogic's forecasting methodology, data sources (Stripe, bank feeds, AR/AP systems), and decision criteria. Lucas walked through specific scenarios where manual forecasts failed. Discussed integration requirements and data refresh frequency needs. Lucas mentioned CEO Levi will need to sign off on any purchase.

questions_asked.question: Beyond forecasting, are there other automation opportunities you're exploring?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we're also looking at automating monthly reporting and potentially some compliance workflows

questions_asked.question: Can your platform handle real-time data feeds or is it batch-based?
questions_asked.answer: Both - you can configure real-time connections for critical data sources and batch updates for others

questions_asked.question: What does success look like for this project in 6 months?
questions_asked.answer: Forecast variance under 5%, automated weekly updates, and Finance team spending 50% less time on manual model building

objections: Need to understand total cost - we're a small team with limited budget
next_steps: Rep to prepare demo focused on forecasting use case
Lucas to gather sample data for demo
Lucas to check Levi's calendar for demo

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent follow-up email recapping discovery discussion and sharing demo agenda. Included links to two fintech customer case studies focused on forecasting accuracy improvements. Lucas confirmed demo scheduled for following week and shared that Levi would join for the first 15 minutes.
next_steps: Demo call scheduled for Tuesday 3pm with Lucas and Levi

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent brief check-in email two days before scheduled demo to confirm attendance and ask if Lucas had any specific scenarios to prioritize. Lucas confirmed attendance but mentioned Levi had a conflict and would join a future call instead. Requested focus on integration capabilities during demo.

questions_asked.question: Can you show how the Stripe integration works specifically?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, will make that a key part of the demo

next_steps: Demo call proceeding with Lucas only
Will reschedule Levi for proposal stage

touches.ai_summary: 14-minute proposal call where rep presented pricing for 4-seat unlimited automation plan at $4,500 annually. Lucas expressed sticker shock and noted this was significantly higher than budgeted. Rep offered to explore scaled implementation or adjusted to $2,500 with extended contract. Lucas appreciated flexibility and said he'd discuss with Levi before moving forward.

questions_asked.question: Is there a tier between basic and unlimited that might fit our needs?
questions_asked.answer: The unlimited plan is the best fit for your forecasting requirements, but I can work with you on pricing given your stage

objections: Budget was around $2,000-2,500 for this type of tool - need to justify the ROI to CEO
next_steps: Rep to send revised proposal at $2,500 ACV
Lucas to review with Levi
Schedule exec alignment call

touches.ai_summary: 27-minute exec meeting with Levi Bennett (CEO) joining to understand strategic value and pricing rationale. Levi asked direct questions about ROI, implementation risk, and why the significant discount from original pricing. Rep positioned the discount as early-stage customer investment. Levi expressed concern about committing to annual contract without proof of value but acknowledged forecasting pain is real.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we don't see the forecasting improvement we need in first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: We include a success milestone in the contract - if core forecasting metrics aren't met by day 90, you have an out clause

questions_asked.question: Why the 44% discount from your standard pricing - is this product unproven at our scale?
questions_asked.answer: Not at all - this is an early customer rate to build fintech references. Standard pricing reflects enterprise deals with different requirements

questions_asked.question: Beyond forecasting, would automating compliance reporting deliver additional value?
questions_asked.answer: Potentially, but let's prove value on forecasting first before expanding scope

objections: Annual commitment feels risky without track record - prefer quarterly initially
next_steps: Rep to add 90-day success clause to contract
Levi to discuss internally with Lucas and finance
Follow-up call in 3 days

touches.ai_summary: 30-minute final exec meeting to finalize terms. Levi and Lucas confirmed they want to move forward at $2,500 ACV with the 90-day success clause. Discussed implementation timeline (targeting 3-week launch), training requirements (2 sessions for finance team), and ongoing support model. Levi requested quarterly business reviews to track ROI. Rep agreed to all terms. Verbal commitment to proceed, contracts to be sent for signature.

questions_asked.question: What does the support model look like - do we have a dedicated contact?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, you'll have a named CSM for implementation and ongoing support, plus access to technical support team

questions_asked.question: When would you ideally want to launch to hit your Q2 planning cycle?
questions_asked.answer: We need to be operational by May 1st to inform Q2 forecast updates

questions_asked.question: Can we add seats mid-contract if we expand the use case to other teams?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely - seats can be added anytime with pro-rated pricing for remainder of term

next_steps: Rep to send contract with agreed terms by EOD
Lucas to coordinate signature from Levi
Implementation kickoff call to be scheduled upon signature
